scope: development-only; frozen-test labels and metrics excluded
threshold_policy: approximately 70% recall; selected using rolling folds 1-3 only and evaluated unchanged on fold 4
decision_rationale: Random Forest leads folds-1-3 mean PR-AUC, four-fold mean PR-AUC, and fold-4 PR-AUC.
Random Forest has the fewest fold-4 false negatives at the model-specific approximately-70%-recall threshold.
Random Forest performance is stable across five fixed seeds.
HistGradientBoosting produces 18 fewer fold-4 false positives but three more false negatives and does not lead PR-AUC.
Logistic regression is simpler but trails both tree models on the completed development comparison.
The TCN is rejected as non-material evidence because its matching-cohort gain is below the retention rule and its fold-4 recall tradeoff is weaker.

xgboost_decision.reason: Skip XGBoost. The bounded sklearn boosted-tree evaluation already establishes that boosting does not surpass Random Forest on development PR-AUC, and another optional implementation would extend model search after sufficient evidence exists to make the development-only decision.
